Do not edit this file directly. -->  # VER-QUIC-RFC8999-0001 - QUIC RFC 8999 Invariants Verification  ## Scope  Verify the RFC 8999 long-header invariant slice, including the shared wire shape used by version negotiation, the connection-ID length rules, and the preservation of opaque version-specific data.  ## Requirements Verified  - REQ-QUIC-RFC8999-S5P1-0001 - REQ-QUIC-RFC8999-S5P1-0002 - REQ-QUIC-RFC8999-S5P1-0003 - REQ-QUIC-RFC8999-S5P1-0004 - REQ-QUIC-RFC8999-S5P1-0005 - REQ-QUIC-RFC8999-S5P1-0006 - REQ-QUIC-RFC8999-S5P1-0007 - REQ-QUIC-RFC8999-S5P1-0008  ## Verification Method  Execution, inspection, fuzzing, and benchmark evidence.  ## Preconditions  - The `Incursa.Quic` solution builds successfully. - The RFC 8999 requirement traits are discoverable by the quality inventory. - The parser, property, fuzz, and benchmark suites remain runnable in the local workbench flow.  ## Procedure or Approach  - Run the RFC 8999 and overlap-focused unit and property tests. - Run the header and version-negotiation fuzz suites. - Capture the long-header parsing benchmark output. - Run the repo-local quality evidence scripts. - Re-run the JSON render and validation checks so the downstream trace surface reflects the updated requirement links.  ## Expected Result  - The parser distinguishes long and short headers correctly. - Truncated inputs fail consistently. - Shared wire-shape tests are attached to the RFC 8999 requirements they prove. - The quality lane surfaces the evidence in the repo-local reports without missing downstream trace or verification links.  ## Evidence  -
